methods.output: 双信号齐全→免疫激活；仅信号1→免疫耐受（anergy）
methods.key_insight: 免疫系统通过要求'上下文证据'（共刺激信号）来区分危险入侵者和自身抗原，而非仅靠单一识别信号
methods.paper: Janeway CA Jr. Approaching the asymptote? Evolution and revolution in immunology. Cold Spring Harb Symp Quant Biol. 1989;54:1-13; Bretscher P, Cohn M. A theory of self-nonself discrimination. Science. 1970;169(3950):1042-1049

transfer_examples.how: 将'双信号激活'映射为：用户身份认证（信号1）+ 上下文二次确认（信号2，如地理位置、设备指纹、行为模式），缺少第二信号时默认拒绝而非放行
transfer_examples.result: 减少账号被盗后的未授权操作，误伤率可控

transfer_examples.how: 模型上线需要'技术评审通过'（信号1）+ '业务风险评估通过'（信号2），缺少任一信号则模型进入等待/搁置状态而非自动上线
transfer_examples.result: 防止仅有技术指标达标但未经业务风险审查的模型进入生产环境

D2.anchor: 胸腺选择同时淘汰反应太弱和太强的 T 细胞，只留下中间可用者

methods.name: Thymic Positive-Negative Selection
methods.mechanism: 在胸腺中，T 细胞通过 TCR 与自身 MHC-抗原的结合强度接受双向筛选：结合过弱的细胞因'阳性选择失败'而凋亡（无法识别自身 MHC），结合过强的细胞因'阴性选择'而被删除（对自身抗原反应过度）。只有结合强度处于中间窗口的细胞存活并输出到外周。
methods.input: 未成熟 T 细胞的 TCR 与胸腺上皮细胞/树突状细胞表面的 MHC-自身抗原复合物
methods.process: ① 未成熟 T 细胞进入胸腺皮质；② 与胸腺上皮细胞接触→阳性选择检验（能否识别自身 MHC）；③ 存活者迁移至髓质→与 DC 接触→阴性选择检验（是否对自身抗原过度反应）；④ 通过两轮筛选的细胞输出为成熟 naive T 细胞
methods.output: 仅保留 TCR 亲和力处于中间范围的 T 细胞克隆（约 2-5% 存活）
methods.key_insight: 免疫系统不是选择'最好的'，而是淘汰两个极端，保留'刚好可用'的中间态——这是对 Goldilocks 原则的生物学实现
methods.paper: Kappler JW, Roehm N, Marrack P. T cell tolerance by clonal elimination in the thymus. Cell. 1987;49(2):273-280

transfer_examples.how: 将'双向淘汰'映射为：候选人能力过低（阳性选择失败，无法胜任基础要求）和过高（阴性选择，如过度特化或文化不匹配）均不录用，只保留能力匹配度在中间窗口的人
transfer_examples.result: 降低'过度资质'导致的留不住人问题，以及'资质不足'导致的绩效问题

transfer_examples.how: 向用户推荐的内容既不能'完全不相关'（亲和力过低，被阳性选择淘汰），也不能'信息茧房化'（亲和力过高，被阴性选择淘汰），只推荐与用户兴趣'有关但有适度新意'的内容
transfer_examples.result: 平衡相关性与多样性，降低用户疲劳和回音室效应

D2.anchor: 免疫检查点如 CTLA-4/PD-1 不是增强攻击，而是在特定阶段刹车

methods.name: Immune Checkpoint Braking
methods.mechanism: CTLA-4 和 PD-1 是 T 细胞表面的抑制性受体。CTLA-4 在免疫应答早期与 CD28 竞争结合 B7 配体，抑制 T 细胞初始激活；PD-1 在外周组织中通过结合 PD-L1/PD-L2 抑制效应 T 细胞功能。两者共同构成免疫系统的'刹车'机制，防止免疫反应过度导致自身免疫损伤。
methods.input: T 细胞活化后的 CTLA-4/PD-1 表达上调 + 配体（B7/PD-L1/PD-L2）在组织中的分布
methods.process: ① T 细胞激活后 CTLA-4 上调→竞争性抑制 CD28-B7 共刺激→限制初始激活强度；② 效应 T 细胞到达外周组织→PD-1 结合 PD-L1→抑制效应功能（增殖、细胞因子释放、杀伤）；③ 免疫应答完成后，检查点信号帮助恢复稳态
methods.output: 免疫应答被限制在可控范围内，避免过度炎症和自身免疫
methods.key_insight: 免疫系统的安全设计不是在启动时过滤，而是在执行阶段动态刹车——这允许快速响应，同时保留随时减速的能力
methods.paper: Ishida Y, Agata Y, Shibahara K, Honjo T. Induced expression of PD-1, a novel member of the immunoglobulin gene superfamily, upon programmed cell death. EMBO J. 1992;11(11):3887-3895; Sharpe AH et al. The function of programmed cell death 1 and its ligands. Nat Immunol. 2007

transfer_examples.how: 系统允许快速扩容（类比免疫激活），但在特定阶段引入'检查点'信号：当资源使用率在 N 分钟后仍高于阈值时触发降级/刹车，而非无限制扩容
transfer_examples.result: 防止因误判或级联效应导致的资源无限扩张（类比细胞因子风暴）

transfer_examples.target: 内容审核/自动决策系统
transfer_examples.how: 自动决策（如封禁、降权）在执行后设有'PD-1 类检查点'：定期用独立机制评估决策是否过当，过当时自动回滚或降级决策强度
transfer_examples.result: 防止自动化系统因模式漂移导致的过度执法

D2.anchor: CRISPR-Cas9 用 guide RNA 定位目标 DNA，再由 Cas9 切割

methods.name: CRISPR-Cas9 Programmable DNA Cleavage
methods.mechanism: CRISPR-Cas9 利用 guide RNA (sgRNA) 的 20nt 序列通过碱基互补配对定位目标 DNA 位点，Cas9 蛋白在识别 PAM 序列（NGG）后对目标链和非目标链进行双链切割（DSB），产生平末端断裂。
methods.input: 20nt guide RNA（与目标 DNA 互补）+ Cas9 蛋白 + 含 PAM 序列的目标 DNA
methods.process: ① sgRNA 与 Cas9 蛋白形成核糖核蛋白复合体；② 复合体扫描 DNA 寻找 PAM 序列（5'-NGG-3'）；③ 发现 PAM 后 sgRNA 与目标链配对形成 R-loop；④ RuvC 结构域切割非目标链，HNH 结构域切割目标链；⑤ 产生双链断裂（DSB）
methods.output: 目标位点的双链 DNA 断裂（DSB），随后由细胞 NHEJ 或 HDR 通路修复
methods.key_insight: 将自然界细菌的适应性免疫系统转化为通用的基因组'查找-替换'工具——只需更换 20nt 的地址即可重新定向
methods.paper: Jinek M, Chylinski K, Fonfara I, Hauer M, Doudna JA, Charpentier E. A Programmable Dual-RNA–Guided DNA Endonuclease in Adaptive Bacterial Immunity. Science. 2012;337(6096):816-821

transfer_examples.how: 设计'定位规则 + 切割/替换操作'的两步编辑模式：先用精确的 pattern 匹配定位记录（类比 sgRNA + PAM），再执行原子化的修改操作（类比 Cas9 切割）
transfer_examples.result: 实现大规模数据中的精确、可编程修改，避免全表扫描

transfer_examples.how: 定义代码模式的'guide pattern'（如 AST 节点序列），工具扫描代码库定位匹配，然后在匹配处执行预定义的重构操作（函数提取、参数重命名等）
transfer_examples.result: 在百万行代码库中实现批量、精准、可重复的代码修改

D2.anchor: PCR 每轮用变性、退火、延伸三步复制目标片段，循环数带来指数放大

methods.name: PCR Three-Step Exponential Amplification
methods.mechanism: PCR 通过温度循环实现 DNA 指数扩增：高温变性（~95°C）使双链 DNA 解链，低温退火（~55°C）使引物结合到互补序列，中温延伸（~72°C）使 DNA 聚合酶从引物处合成新链。每轮循环理论上将目标片段数量翻倍，n 轮循环产生约 2^n 倍扩增。
methods.input: 含目标片段的 DNA 模板 + 正向/反向引物 + DNA 聚合酶 + dNTPs
methods.process: ① 变性（94-95°C，15-30s）：双链 DNA 解链为单链；② 退火（50-65°C，15-60s）：引物与目标序列互补结合；③ 延伸（72°C，根据片段长度）：Taq 聚合酶从引物 3' 端合成新链；④ 重复 25-35 个循环
methods.output: 目标 DNA 片段的指数级拷贝（理论上 2^n，实际略低）
methods.key_insight: 用温度作为唯一控制信号，通过三步循环将分子识别（引物退火）和信号放大（聚合酶延伸）耦合在同一个反应中——极简设计产生极强效果
methods.paper: Mullis KB, Faloona FA, Scharf S, Saiki R, Horn G, Erlich H. Specific enzymatic amplification of DNA in vitro: the polymerase chain reaction. Cold Spring Harb Symp Quant Biol. 1986;51:263-273

transfer_examples.how: 设计'三阶段循环放大'模式：① 触发（类比变性：释放信号源）→ ② 识别（类比退火：特异性匹配目标模式）→ ③ 放大（类比延伸：生成完整报警事件）。每轮循环将微弱信号指数放大为可检测事件
transfer_examples.result: 在噪声环境中检测极低频但重要的异常模式

transfer_examples.how: 对有潜力的实验变体采用'指数放量'策略：每轮观察周期确认指标达标后流量翻倍，类似 PCR 的指数扩增，快速从微量流量验证到全量部署
transfer_examples.result: 缩短实验周期，同时保留逐步验证的安全性

D2.anchor: Rt 大于 1 时传播扩张，小于 1 时传播收缩；干预目标是把 Rt 压到阈值以下

methods.name: Effective Reproduction Number Rt Threshold Control
methods.mechanism: 有效再生数 Rt 定义为在时间 t 平均每个感染者能传染的二代病例数。Rt > 1 表示疫情扩张，Rt < 1 表示疫情收缩，Rt = 1 为临界点。Wallinga-Teunis 方法利用世代间隔分布和病例发病时间对来估计时变 Rt。
methods.input: 病例发病时间序列 + 世代间隔分布（generation interval distribution）
methods.process: ① 收集每个病例的发病时间；② 根据世代间隔分布计算每对(i,j)病例间 j 被 i 传染的概率 p_{ij}；③ 对每个时间窗口内的病例求和得到 Rt 估计值；④ 监测 Rt 与阈值 1 的关系，决定干预力度
methods.output: 时变有效再生数 Rt 的点估计及置信区间
methods.key_insight: 不需要知道完整传播链，仅通过病例时间分布和世代间隔的统计特性就能实时估计传播趋势——这是一个'用可观测变量推断隐状态'的典型方法
methods.paper: Wallinga J, Teunis P. Different epidemic curves for SARS reveal similar impacts of control measures through generation intervals. Proc Natl Acad Sci USA. 2004;101(14):5032-5037
methods.code: https://github.com/covid-19-Re/shiny-dailyRe (Rt 实时估计) ; R 包 EpiEstim

transfer_examples.target: 谣言/虚假信息传播监控
transfer_examples.how: 定义信息的'再生数'：一条内容被平均转发/引用的次数。当 Rt > 1 时标记为'活跃传播'并触发干预（标记、降权、fact-check），目标是将 Rt 压到 1 以下
transfer_examples.result: 在虚假信息爆发早期识别并遏制传播，避免全面删除带来的对抗

transfer_examples.how: 监测代码库中'低质量代码'的扩散速率：每个 bad pattern 被新代码引用/复制的平均次数（类比 Rt）。当 Rt > 1 时集中资源重构关键节点，将 Rt 压到 1 以下
transfer_examples.result: 优先处理扩散最快的技术债，而非均匀投入资源

D2.anchor: 贝叶斯诊断先有 pre-test probability，再用似然比更新成 post-test probability

methods.name: Bayesian Diagnostic Reasoning
methods.mechanism: 贝叶斯诊断将临床推理形式化为概率更新过程：从先验概率（pre-test probability，基于流行病学和临床表现）出发，用检查结果的似然比（likelihood ratio）更新得到后验概率（post-test probability）。公式：后验优势 = 先验优势 x 似然比。
methods.input: 疾病先验概率（基于人群流行率、病史、体征）+ 诊断检查的似然比（LR+ 或 LR-）
methods.process: ① 估计 pre-test probability（如根据年龄、症状、危险因素）；② 将概率转换为优势（odds = p/(1-p)）；③ 乘以检查结果的似然比得到 post-test odds；④ 将 post-test odds 转回概率；⑤ 可串联多个检查重复更新
methods.output: 更新后的疾病概率（post-test probability），用于决定是否进一步检查或开始治疗
methods.key_insight: 没有任何单一检查结果能独立解读——同一个阳性结果在不同先验概率下产生截然不同的后验概率（'低基础率阳性结果的假阳性陷阱'）
methods.paper: Sackett DL, Haynes RB, Guyatt GH, Tugwell P. Clinical Epidemiology: A Basic Science for Clinical Medicine. 2nd ed. Little, Brown; 1991

transfer_examples.how: 安全告警的处理采用贝叶斯推理：先估计'该类告警为真实攻击的先验概率'（基于历史数据），再用检测工具的灵敏度/特异度计算似然比，更新得到该告警为真实攻击的后验概率
transfer_examples.result: 大幅降低误报率（大量低先验告警的后验概率仍然很低），集中精力处理高后验概率的告警

transfer_examples.how: 新功能立项前设定'该功能成功'的先验概率（基于市场数据），然后用用户调研、MVP 测试等结果的似然比逐步更新概率，直到后验概率超过'立项阈值'或降至'放弃阈值'
transfer_examples.result: 避免因单一测试结果（正面或负面）而仓促决策，实现证据的有序累积

D2.anchor: START triage 先问能否行走，再看呼吸、灌注、意识，按颜色分诊

methods.name: START Mass Casualty Triage
methods.mechanism: START (Simple Triage and Rapid Treatment) 通过一系列快速二元判断将伤员分为四类：能行走→绿色（轻微）；不能行走→检查呼吸→无呼吸→重新体位后仍无→黑色（死亡/不可救）；有呼吸→检查灌注（桡动脉搏动或毛细血管再充盈）和意识→按结果分红色（立即）或黄色（延迟）。全程 60 秒内完成。
methods.input: 大规模伤亡场景中的伤员集合
methods.process: ① 指令'能走的走到指定区域'→绿色标签；② 对不能走者检查呼吸：无呼吸→开放气道后仍无→黑色标签；③ 有呼吸→检查桡动脉搏动/毛细血管再充盈（>2秒→红色，控制出血）；④ 灌注正常→检查意识（按简单指令执行→黄色；不能→红色）
methods.output: 四色标签分类：红色（立即处理）、黄色（可延迟）、绿色（轻微）、黑色（死亡/不可救）
methods.key_insight: 分诊不追求准确诊断，而是在资源极度有限下用最少步骤做出'足够好'的分类决策——先处理决策树中最容易判断的分支（能否行走），再逐步细化
methods.paper: Benson M, Koenig KL, Schultz CH. Disaster triage: START, then SAVE — a new method of dynamic triage for victims of a catastrophic earthquake. Prehosp Disaster Med. 1996;11(2):117-124
methods.code: https://github.com/sdether/hazmat-triage (分诊辅助工具概念实现)

transfer_examples.how: 事故初现时用 START 式快速判断树：① 影响用户能否自行恢复（类比'能否行走'）→ 绿色/观察；② 不能恢复→检查核心服务是否存活（类比'呼吸'）→ 不存活为最高级别；③ 服务存活→检查数据一致性/影响范围（类比'灌注和意识'）→ 分红/黄
transfer_examples.result: 在大规模事故中 60 秒内完成分级，避免因分析过久而延迟最高优先级的响应

transfer_examples.how: 客服或研发工单系统用二元判断树快速分类：① 用户能否自助解决→低优先级；② 不能→是否影响核心流程→是则紧急；③ 不影响核心→是否影响数据→按结果分优先级
transfer_examples.result: 在工单量激增时快速分配资源，减少分诊时间

D2.anchor: STDP 由突触前后放电的先后毫秒差决定增强还是削弱连接

methods.name: Spike-Timing-Dependent Plasticity (STDP)
methods.mechanism: 突触前后神经元的放电时间差（毫秒级）决定突触连接强度的变化方向和幅度：突触前先放电（Δt > 0，前领先后 0-20ms）→ 突触增强（LTP）；突触后先放电（Δt < 0，后领先前 0-20ms）→ 突触减弱（LTD）。变化量随时间差呈指数衰减。
methods.input: 突触前和突触后神经元的动作电位时间戳（毫秒精度）
methods.process: ① 记录突触前动作电位到达时间 t_pre 和突触后动作电位时间 t_post；② 计算 Δt = t_post - t_pre；③ 若 Δt > 0（前先于后）→ 突触权重增加，幅度 = A+ * exp(-Δt/τ+)；④ 若 Δt < 0（后先于前）→ 突触权重减少，幅度 = -A- * exp(Δt/τ-)；⑤ 窗口约 ±20ms
methods.output: 突触权重的双向调节（增强或减弱），实现时间编码的学习规则
methods.key_insight: 因果关系由时间顺序编码：'因'在'果'之前→加强连接；'果'在'因'之前→削弱连接。这是一种极其精简的因果学习规则
methods.paper: Bi GQ, Poo MM. Synaptic modifications in cultured hippocampal neurons: dependence on spike timing, synaptic strength, and postsynaptic cell type. J Neurosci. 1998;18(24):10464-10472
methods.code: https://github.com/NeuralEnsemble/PyNN (支持 STDP 的神经模拟); https://brian2.readthedocs.io (STDP 模型实现)

transfer_examples.how: 在事件流中应用 STDP 式时间窗口规则：事件 A 在事件 B 之前发生（窗口内）→ 加强 A→B 的因果权重；A 在 B 之后→减弱权重。权重长期累积形成因果图
transfer_examples.result: 从时序数据中自动发现因果关系，无需预先假设因果方向

transfer_examples.target: 强化学习的时序信用分配
transfer_examples.how: 将 STDP 的时间衰减窗口应用于奖励信号的信用分配：距离奖励越近的行为获得越强的正向更新，距离越远的行为更新越弱（指数衰减），方向由时间顺序决定
transfer_examples.result: 在稀疏奖励环境中实现更精细的信用分配，避免将奖励均匀归因于所有历史行为

D2.anchor: 匝道控制不让车随便进主路，而是按主路状态限制流入

methods.name: ALINEA Ramp Metering
methods.mechanism: 匝道控制（Ramp Metering）通过在匝道入口设置信号灯，根据主路下游交通状态动态调节进入主路的车流量。ALINEA 算法使用比例-积分（PI）反馈控制：匝道流量调整量与'期望占有率 - 实测下游占有率'的差值成正比，从而将主路交通密度维持在临界值附近，防止自由流变为拥堵。
methods.input: 主路下游实时占有率 o_out + 期望占有率 Ô（设定值）+ 前一时刻匝道放行率 r(k-1)
methods.process: ① 传感器采集主路下游实时占有率 o_out(k)；② 计算误差 e(k) = Ô - o_out(k)；③ 更新匝道放行率：r(k) = r(k-1) + K_R * e(k)；④ 将 r(k) 转换为匝道信号灯的红绿时间比；⑤ 持续循环调节
methods.output: 实时调节的匝道放行率，维持主路交通流在自由流/临界流状态
methods.key_insight: 不是试图控制所有车辆，而是控制'入口阀门'——通过限制流入来保护主路系统的整体性能。局部牺牲（匝道排队）换取全局收益（主路畅通）
methods.paper: Papageorgiou M, Hadj-Salem H, Blosseville JM. ALINEA: A local feedback control law for on-ramp metering. Transportation Research Record 1320. 1991:58-64

transfer_examples.how: 在 API 网关实现 ALINEA 式反馈限流：监测下游服务的响应时间/错误率（类比 o_out），设定目标阈值（类比 Ô），动态调节上游准入请求速率（类比 r(k)），而非使用固定限流阈值
transfer_examples.result: 在后端服务性能波动时自适应调节流量，避免因固定阈值过松导致过载或过严导致浪费

transfer_examples.how: 团队根据当前进行中的任务负载（类比主路占有率）动态调节新任务接入速率：负载高于阈值时减少新任务准入（类比匝道红灯），负载低于阈值时增加准入，而非在任何时候都接受所有任务
transfer_examples.result: 避免团队过载导致的交付质量下降，通过入口控制保持可持续的吞吐量

D2.anchor: Braess 悖论：加一条路在某些需求区间反而让整体通行成本上升

methods.name: Braess 悖论
methods.mechanism: 在用户均衡（Nash 均衡）条件下，向网络添加一条新路径反而可能增加所有用户的总出行成本。每个个体选择最短路径的理性行为导致集体非最优结果。
methods.input: 交通网络拓扑（节点、边、成本函数），OD 需求矩阵
methods.process: 每条边的成本是该边上流量的递增函数；用户独立选择最短路径直至达到均衡；此时新增一条零拥堵边后，原有两条路径上的用户同时涌向新边，导致所有路径成本均上升
methods.output: 新增边后的网络总出行时间高于新增前；均衡流量分布
methods.key_insight: 个体最优不等于系统最优——网络结构改变会重新分配外部性；因此修路前需要做博弈分析而非仅看几何距离
methods.paper: Braess, D. (1968). Über ein Paradoxon aus der Verkehrsplanung. Unternehmensforschung, 12, 258-268.

transfer_examples.how: 在数据中心网络中添加一条低延迟链路，导致所有流量涌向该链路，总延迟反而上升
transfer_examples.result: 网络工程师在路由策略中引入全局拥塞感知，模拟系统最优而非用户均衡

transfer_examples.how: 引入一个新物种（新路径）改变了资源竞争格局，原有物种的适应度均下降
transfer_examples.result: 入侵物种管理中需考虑博弈论视角下的间接竞争效应

D2.anchor: LWR 模型把车流当作波，拥堵会像冲击波一样向后传播

methods.name: LWR 运动波模型
methods.mechanism: 将交通流视为一维可压缩流体，用守恒方程 ∂ρ/∂t + ∂q/∂x = 0 描述车辆守恒。流量 q 与密度 ρ 之间存在基本图关系 q = f(ρ)，扰动以运动波形式传播，拥堵形成冲击波。
methods.input: 路段密度分布 ρ(x,t)，基本图 q-ρ 关系
methods.process: 采用特征线法求解偏微分方程；自由流区域特征线向前传播，拥堵区域特征线向后传播；密度间断面即为冲击波，波速由 Rankine-Hugoniot 条件 Δq/Δρ 确定
methods.output: 密度时空图，冲击波传播速度与方向，排队形成与消散过程
methods.key_insight: 拥堵像声波一样从瓶颈处向后游走——理解这一点就能预判排队长度和清散时间
methods.paper: Lighthill, M.J. & Whitham, G.B. (1955). On Kinematic Waves II. A Theory of Traffic Flow on Long Crowded Roads. Proceedings of the Royal Society A, 229(1178), 317-345. 亦见 Richards, P.I. (1956). Shockwaves on the Highway. Operations Research, 4(1), 42-51.

transfer_examples.how: 将工件在传送带上的流动建模为连续流体，瓶颈工位产生向上传播的阻塞冲击波
transfer_examples.result: 用 LWR 方法预测在制品堆积位置，优化缓冲区容量配置

transfer_examples.how: 互联网拥塞控制中数据包流符合守恒方程，丢包点形成冲击波
transfer_examples.result: 启发了 TCP 拥塞控制中的速率调整策略设计

D2.anchor: 双环相位控制中，冲突相位不能同时放行，必须跨 barrier 切换

methods.name: NEMA 双环相位控制
methods.mechanism: 将 8 个信号相位分配到两个独立运行的环（ring）中，每环内相位顺序执行。两个环通过 barrier 分割为两组，同一 barrier 组内的相位可独立计时，但跨 barrier 切换时两个环必须同时完成当前组。
methods.input: 交叉口各进口道转向需求、冲突矩阵、最小绿灯时间和黄灯间隔
methods.process: 将冲突相位分入不同环以避免同时放行；环内按顺序推进相位；在 barrier 处强制同步——只有两个环都完成当前组的最后一个相位后，才能进入下一组
methods.output: 信号配时方案（各相位绿灯起止时刻），绿灯间隔矩阵
methods.key_insight: 用环和 barrier 的拓扑约束来管理冲突——同一环内不会同时放行冲突流，barrier 保证对向冲突流之间的安全间隔
methods.paper: NEMA TS 2-2003 (v02.06). Traffic Controller Assemblies with NTCIP Requirements. National Electrical Manufacturers Association.

transfer_examples.how: 将互斥任务分入不同 ring 并行推进，在 barrier 处同步，避免死锁
transfer_examples.result: 借鉴双环 barrier 结构设计了可预测延迟的实时调度器

transfer_examples.how: 不同团队并行准备，只有所有团队完成准备阶段后才进入手术阶段
transfer_examples.result: 减少了手术室周转时间中的同步等待

D2.anchor: 防火/防烟分区通过隔断、门和压差限制烟火蔓延，局部失火不应全楼扩散

methods.name: Fire Compartmentalization（防火/防烟分区）
methods.mechanism: 把大空间切成若干隔间，用防火墙、防火门和压差控制连通。正压隔间的空气往负压隔间流，保证烟气只往一个方向走、不会反向蔓延。某个隔间起火时，结构保证火与烟被困在本区内。
methods.input: 建筑空间布局
methods.process: 划分隔间 → 安装防火隔断/门 → 设定压差 → 联动排烟系统
methods.output: 局部失火被限制在本区，相邻区安全
methods.key_insight: 不是消灭火灾，而是控制火灾的传播范围——牺牲局部保全整体
methods.paper: NFPA 80A, NFPA 101 Life Safety Code; BS 9999

transfer_examples.target: 软件工程-微服务 Bulkhead 模式
transfer_examples.how: 每个微服务有独立的资源池（线程池/连接池），一个服务故障不会耗尽其他服务的资源
transfer_examples.result: Netflix Hystrix 的核心设计模式

transfer_examples.how: 把网络切成 VLAN/安全区，一个区被入侵不蔓延到其他区
transfer_examples.result: 零信任架构的基础原则

transfer_examples.how: 不同资产类别放在独立的账户/SPV 中，一个类别爆雷不传染其他
transfer_examples.result: 资产证券化和风险隔离的标准结构

D2.anchor: 抗震耗能构件允许指定部位先屈服，把破坏集中到可替换部件

methods.name: 抗震耗能构件
methods.mechanism: 在结构中预设可替换的耗能装置（阻尼器、屈服支撑、摩擦连接件），地震时这些装置优先发生塑性变形或摩擦耗散能量，保护主体结构保持弹性。
methods.input: 结构动力模型、地震动输入（加速度时程）、耗能装置的力-位移滞回曲线
methods.process: 耗能装置安装在预期大变形位置（支撑、节点、层间）；地震输入能量经装置滞回循环转化为热能耗散；主体构件应力被限制在弹性范围内；震后仅更换耗能装置即可恢复使用
methods.output: 层间位移角减小量、基底剪力降低量、结构损伤指数
methods.key_insight: 把破坏集中到可替换的'保险丝'上——用局部牺牲换取整体安全，震后修复成本大幅降低
methods.paper: Soong, T.T. & Spencer Jr., B.F. (2002). Supplemental energy dissipation: state-of-the-art and state-of-the-practice. Engineering Structures, 24(3), 243-259.

transfer_examples.how: 在微服务架构中设置熔断器，故障请求被熔断器截断，保护核心服务不被级联故障拖垮
transfer_examples.result: Netflix Hystrix 等框架将耗能构件思想迁移到分布式系统容错设计

transfer_examples.how: 设置独立的合规或风控部门作为'缓冲层'，先吸收和消化外部冲击
transfer_examples.result: 业务部门保持稳定运营，风控部门集中处理监管变化和违规风险

D2.anchor: 应力强度因子超过材料断裂韧度时，裂纹会继续扩展

methods.name: 应力强度因子断裂准则
methods.mechanism: 裂纹尖端附近的应力场可由单一参数 K（应力强度因子）完整描述。当 K 达到材料的临界值 Kc（断裂韧度）时，裂纹失稳扩展。
methods.input: 远场应力 σ、裂纹长度 a、几何修正系数 Y（K = Yσ√(πa)）
methods.process: 根据裂纹几何形状和加载方式选择 K 的表达式（I/II/III 型）；计算当前工况下的 K 值；与材料测试获得的 Kc 比较；K < Kc 则安全，K ≥ Kc 则裂纹扩展
methods.output: 安全裕度 Kc/K，裂纹扩展判断，剩余寿命估算
methods.key_insight: 裂纹尖端的破坏由一个数字 K 决定——它把复杂的应力集中问题简化为一个可测量、可比较的标量
methods.paper: Irwin, G.R. (1957). Analysis of Stresses and Strains Near the End of a Crack Traversing a Plate. Journal of Applied Mechanics, 24, 361-364.

transfer_examples.how: 用 Paris 公式 da/dN = C(ΔK)^m 将应力强度因子与裂纹扩展速率关联，预测零件剩余疲劳寿命
transfer_examples.result: 航空发动机叶片检测间隔从定期更换变为基于裂纹增长的按需维护

transfer_examples.how: 将团队内部冲突的'应力集中'类比为裂纹尖端，冲突强度超过团队韧度时'裂纹'扩展为离职或分裂
transfer_examples.result: 管理学中引入'组织韧度'概念，通过制度设计提高临界阈值

D2.anchor: 调蓄池先截留峰值径流，再通过受限出口慢慢释放，削平下游洪峰

methods.name: 调蓄池削峰
methods.mechanism: 在排水系统中设置蓄水容量，暴雨期间先暂时截留峰值径流，降低出水流量至下游管网承载能力以下，然后在洪峰过后缓慢释放存水。
methods.input: 设计暴雨强度过程线、汇水面积、下游管网允许排放流量、调蓄池容积
methods.process: 暴雨开始后径流经进水口进入调蓄池；池内水位上升，出水口按设计的小流量（孔口/堰控制）释放；洪峰期间大量水量被暂时存储；雨停后池内积水继续缓慢排出直至排空
methods.output: 下游出口峰值流量削减比、调蓄池最大蓄水量、排空时间
methods.key_insight: 用空间换时间——把瞬时的大流量转化为时间上的延迟释放，用有限容积换取峰值削减
methods.paper: USDA Natural Resources Conservation Service. National Engineering Handbook, Part 630 Hydrology. 亦见 Viessman, W. & Lewis, G.L. (2003). Introduction to Hydrology, 5th ed., Pearson.

transfer_examples.how: I/O 系统中用缓冲队列暂存突发写入请求，再以稳定速率写入磁盘
transfer_examples.result: 削平写入峰值，保护磁盘免受随机突发冲击

transfer_examples.how: 在需求峰值期间动用安全库存，峰值过后逐步补货恢复库存水平
transfer_examples.result: 避免产能过载，保持生产节拍稳定

D2.anchor: 空间句法把空间连通关系变成图，研究可达性如何影响行为

methods.name: 空间句法
methods.mechanism: 将建筑或城市空间的连通关系抽象为图结构，用凸空间或轴线作为节点、视线或通道作为边，通过图论指标（整合度、选择度、深度）量化每个空间的可达性与人流潜力。
methods.input: 建筑/城市平面图、空间划分方案（凸空间或最长轴线集合）
methods.process: 将平面图转化为凸空间划分或轴线地图；构建连接图；计算每个节点的整合度（到其他所有节点的平均最短距离的倒数）和选择度（经过该节点的最短路径数）；与实测人流数据回归验证
methods.output: 整合度/选择度热力图、人流预测方程、空间可达性排名
methods.key_insight: 空间的几何形状和连通结构先验地决定了人流模式——设计空间就是设计行为
methods.paper: Hillier, B. & Hanson, J. (1984). The Social Logic of Space. Cambridge University Press.

transfer_examples.how: 将模块调用关系建模为图，用整合度识别核心模块、用选择度识别关键桥梁模块
transfer_examples.result: 识别出架构中的高耦合热点和高介数瓶颈，指导重构优先级

transfer_examples.how: 将个人社交网络拓扑映射为'社交空间'，计算各节点的整合度
transfer_examples.result: 高整合度节点对应意见领袖，高选择度节点对应信息经纪人

D2.anchor: 欲望路径记录真实行走路线；正式道路若不贴合，会被脚印纠正

methods.name: 欲望路径
methods.mechanism: 行人自发踩踏形成的非正式小径，反映真实行走需求与设计路径之间的偏差。这些路径是用户行为的自然实验数据，揭示了最短路或最高效路线。
methods.input: 场地中正式道路系统与实际行人轨迹（可通过观察、GPS 追踪或草皮磨损程度获取）
methods.process: 记录被踩踏出的非正式路径的起点、终点和走向；与现有规划道路对比；识别设计与需求之间的系统性偏差；将高频欲望路径纳入下一轮正式道路设计
methods.output: 欲望路径热力图、设计修正建议、用户真实 OD 需求
methods.key_insight: 不要对抗用户行为——先让行为发生，再用设计响应行为。用脚投票比问卷更诚实
methods.paper: 无单一经典论文。概念源自景观建筑与城市规划实践传统，常与 John Brinckerhoff Jackson 的景观写作和芬兰建筑教育中'先不铺路'的做法关联。参见路径依赖设计（path-dependent design）相关文献。
methods.code: 无标准库；可用 GPS 轨迹聚类实现，参见 https://github.com/scikit-mobility/scikit-mobility

transfer_examples.how: 监控用户实际调用模式（日志分析），发现用户频繁绕过官方接口组合使用内部方法
transfer_examples.result: 将高频调用模式封装为正式 API 端点——这是软件世界的'铺路'

transfer_examples.how: 观察员工实际工作流而非纸面流程，发现大量绕行和变通做法
transfer_examples.result: 将实际高效做法固化为正式流程，取消无人遵守的官僚环节

D2.anchor: 岛屿物种数由迁入率和灭绝率共同决定，面积和隔离度改变平衡点

methods.name: 岛屿生物地理学平衡理论
methods.mechanism: 岛屿上的物种数由迁入率（新物种到达）和灭绝率（现有物种消失）的动态平衡决定。迁入率随岛屿距大陆距离增大而下降；灭绝率随岛屿面积减小而上升。两条曲线的交点即为平衡物种数。
methods.input: 岛屿面积、岛屿与物种源地的距离、迁入率函数、灭绝率函数
methods.process: 距大陆近的岛屿迁入率高（物种容易到达），远的低；大岛屿灭绝率低（种群大、栖息地多样），小的高；随着岛上物种数增加，迁入率下降（因为新到的物种更可能是已有的），灭绝率上升（种间竞争加剧）；两条曲线交叉处达到平衡
methods.output: 平衡物种数 S*、物种周转率（迁入-灭绝频率）、面积-物种数关系曲线
methods.key_insight: 生态群落不是静态清单而是动态平衡——保护区设计必须同时考虑面积和连通性
methods.paper: MacArthur, R.H. & Wilson, E.O. (1967). The Theory of Island Biogeography. Princeton University Press. (Monographs in Population Biology)

transfer_examples.target: 技术生态系统的知识多样性
transfer_examples.how: 将小众编程语言社区视为'岛屿'，大语言社区视为'大陆'；社区越小（面积小）越容易丢失贡献者（灭绝），离主流越远（隔离度高）越难获得新贡献者（迁入）
transfer_examples.result: 开源社区管理策略：保持足够活跃度（面积）并降低参与门槛（距离）

transfer_examples.how: 商业街的小店铺构成'岛屿群落'，客流是'大陆迁入源'，租金是'灭绝压力'
transfer_examples.result: 解释了为什么大型商业综合体内部的独立小店更替率与其面积和位置的关系

D2.anchor: 适度扰动可以提高多样性；太少或太多扰动都会降低多样性

methods.name: 中度干扰假说
methods.mechanism: 当干扰频率或强度处于中等水平时，物种多样性达到峰值。低干扰下优势物种竞争排斥其他物种导致多样性低；高干扰下只有少数耐受性强的机会种能存活。中等干扰既阻止竞争排斥，又不致毁灭整个群落。
methods.input: 干扰频率/强度梯度、群落组成数据
methods.process: 沿干扰梯度采样群落多样性（物种丰富度或 Shannon 指数）；低干扰端：竞争排斥使优势种垄断资源，多样性下降；高干扰端：环境剧烈变化淘汰大部分物种，多样性下降；中等干扰：快拓殖种和竞争优势种共存，多样性最大
methods.output: 干扰强度-多样性倒U型曲线、最优干扰频率区间
methods.key_insight: 秩序和混乱之间的中间态最具创造力——太少扰动导致僵化，太多导致崩溃
methods.paper: Connell, J.H. (1978). Diversity in tropical rain forests and coral reefs. Science, 199(4335), 1302-1310.
methods.code: 无专用库；可用 vegan 包计算多样性指数: https://github.com/vegandevs/vegan

transfer_examples.how: 团队重组频率过低导致思维固化，过高导致无法积累经验，中等频率的团队轮换最大化创新产出
transfer_examples.result: 谷歌和亚马逊等公司实践了'适度动荡'的组织设计

transfer_examples.how: 完全没有准入壁垒的市场由少数巨头垄断（低干扰）；壁垒极高的市场无人能进入（高干扰）；适度壁垒维持竞争者多样性
transfer_examples.result: 反垄断政策设计中引入中度干扰视角，寻找最优监管强度

D2.anchor: 关键种移除会触发级联改变，小角色可能控制大结构

methods.name: Keystone Species Removal Cascade
methods.mechanism: 在食物网中，某个物种（关键种）对群落结构的影响力远超其生物量占比；移除它会触发竞争排斥和营养级联，导致多样性骤降。
methods.input: 一个包含多物种的生态食物网，以及待移除的候选关键种（通常为顶级捕食者或强烈竞争者）。
methods.process: 1) 选择实验/观测区域，标记关键种并记录基线物种多样性与优势度；2) 通过物理移除或自然排除去除关键种；3) 长期跟踪群落组成变化，监测优势种扩张、竞争排斥和营养级联效应；4) 对比对照区域，量化物种丰富度下降幅度与群落结构重组模式。
methods.output: 关键种移除后的群落多样性变化曲线、新优势种的确立时间、以及食物网拓扑结构的级联改变图谱。
methods.key_insight: 小角色可能控制大结构——少数低丰度物种通过自上而下的压制维持整个群落多样性，移除它们比移除优势种影响更大。
methods.paper: Paine, R.T. (1966). Food Web Complexity and Species Diversity. The American Naturalist, 100(910), 65-75.
methods.code: https://github.com/PredictiveEcology/SpaDES (生态模拟框架，可建模物种移除级联)

transfer_examples.how: 将关键种移除实验类比为移除组织中的'枢纽人物'（如跨部门协调者），观察信息流断裂和协作网络瓦解。
transfer_examples.result: 提前识别并保护组织中的低可见但高连接度角色，防止协作生态崩溃。

transfer_examples.how: 在微服务架构中模拟'关键服务'下线，观察依赖它的下游服务级联失败。
transfer_examples.result: 指导架构中的熔断和降级策略设计，优先加固高扇出低流量的基础设施服务。

D2.anchor: Adaptive cycle 有增长、保守、释放、重组四阶段；崩塌后可能重组

methods.name: Adaptive Cycle (Panarchy Framework)
methods.mechanism: 复杂适应系统沿四阶段循环演进：快速增长(r)、保守积累(K)、创造性释放(Ω)、重组更新(α)；崩塌不是终点而是重组为新形态的起点。
methods.input: 一个经历长期演化的复杂系统（生态系统、经济体、组织），其可量化的资源积累、连接度和韧性指标。
methods.process: 1) 识别系统当前所处阶段（通过资源积累速率、内部连接密度、创新活力等指标）；2) 在K阶段警惕过度刚性和'记忆锁定'，预留冗余；3) 在Ω阶段不急于恢复原状，而是释放束缚资源、容忍短期混乱；4) 在α阶段鼓励实验和新组合，利用'记忆'（大尺度结构保留）和'反叛'（小尺度创新上传）完成重组。
methods.output: 系统在四阶段循环中的位置诊断、韧性/脆弱性评估、以及重组方向建议。
methods.key_insight: 崩塌（Ω阶段）是系统韧性的必要组成——试图永远维持K阶段的稳定反而会积累更大崩溃风险；真正的韧性来自接受周期并管理重组质量。
methods.paper: Holling, C.S. (1986). The resilience of terrestrial ecosystems. In: Local and Global. Sustainable Development of the Biosphere. Cambridge Univ. Press; Gunderson, L.H. & Holling, C.S. (2002). Panarchy: Understanding Transformations in Human and Natural Systems. Island Press.

transfer_examples.how: 将产品生命周期映射为自适应循环：增长期快速获客、保守期流程固化、危机期市场颠覆、重组期探索新品类。
transfer_examples.result: 在保守期主动引入小规模实验，避免在市场剧变时整个产品线崩溃而无重组弹药。

transfer_examples.how: 将职业生涯视为自适应循环：技能积累(K)阶段的舒适区锁定会在行业变革(Ω)时造成阵痛，但保留跨领域知识(α)加速重组。
transfer_examples.result: 有意识地在稳定期维持学习多样度，将职业转型视为α重组而非失败。

D2.anchor: 牛鞭效应：需求信息向上游传递时波动被放大

methods.name: Bullwhip Effect Analysis
methods.mechanism: 需求信号沿供应链从下游向上游传递时，各节点独立进行需求预测和安全库存决策，导致订单波动逐级放大（方差递增），上游实际面对的需求波动远大于终端消费者的真实波动。
methods.input: 多级供应链各节点的订单历史数据、各节点的预测方法、订货批量策略、提前期信息。
methods.process: 1) 收集零售商→批发商→分销商→制造商各层的订单时间序列；2) 计算各层需求方差与终端消费者需求方差的比值，量化放大程度；3) 诊断放大来源：需求预测更新、订单批量、价格波动、配给博弈四类成因；4) 设计对策：信息共享平台、VMI（供应商管理库存）、每日低价(EDLP)、缩短提前期。
methods.output: 各供应链层级的波动放大系数、主要成因归因图、以及信息共享前后的方差对比模拟。
methods.key_insight: 需求信息不是'原样传递'的——每经过一个独立决策节点都会被该节点的预测逻辑和风险规避扭曲，信息共享是缓解放大的关键但非唯一解。
methods.paper: Lee, H.L., Padmanabhan, V., & Whang, S. (1997). The Bullwhip Effect in Supply Chains. Sloan Management Review, 38(3), 93-102. (Reprinted in Management Science, 50(12), 2004, 1875-1886.)
methods.code: https://github.com/topics/beer-game (Beer Game 仿真，经典牛鞭效应教学工具)

transfer_examples.how: 将新闻事件沿转发链传播类比为供应链：每一层KOL加入自己的解读（预测），导致情绪强度逐级放大。
transfer_examples.result: 设计信息溯源和去噪机制，在传播链中引入'信息共享'节点（事实核查）抑制情绪牛鞭效应。

transfer_examples.how: 部门预算需求沿层级上报时，每层管理者添加安全裕量，导致顶层收到的需求信号远大于真实需要。
transfer_examples.result: 推行零基预算或信息透明的滚动预测，减少各层独立加成导致的预算牛鞭。

D2.anchor: Newsvendor 用缺货成本和滞销成本的比值决定一次性订货分位数

methods.name: Newsvendor Critical Fractile
methods.mechanism: 面对一次性订货决策和随机需求时，最优订货量由缺货的边际惩罚成本(Cu)与滞销的边际过剩成本(Co)的比值决定：当累积需求分布F(Q*)= Cu/(Cu+Co)时，期望总成本最小。
methods.input: 单位售价(p)、单位成本(c)、残值(s)、需求的概率分布F（均值μ、标准差σ或经验分布）。
methods.process: 1) 计算单位缺货成本 Cu = p - c（损失的边际利润）；2) 计算单位滞销成本 Co = c - s（未售出单位的净损失）；3) 计算临界分位数 CR = Cu / (Cu + Co)；4) 在需求分布F上取逆函数：Q* = F⁻¹(CR)；5) 敏感性分析：变动售价、残值或需求分布形状，观察最优订货量的位移。
methods.output: 最优一次性订货量Q*、对应的期望利润/损失、以及临界分位数对参数的敏感度曲线。
methods.key_insight: 最优解不是'最可能的需求'（众数或均值），而是由成本不对称性决定的分位数——当缺货成本远高于滞销成本时，即使均值需求很低，最优订货也可能偏向高分位。
methods.paper: Arrow, K.J., Harris, T., & Marschak, J. (1951). Optimal Inventory Policy. Econometrica, 19(3), 250-272.
methods.code: https://github.com/scipy/scipy (scipy.stats.norm.ppf 可直接计算正态需求下的Q*)

transfer_examples.how: 将云实例预留类比为一次性订货：预留过多闲置浪费(Co)，预留不足需按需补购(Cu)。用历史负载分布和价格差计算最优预留量。
transfer_examples.result: 某企业将预留实例比例从经验性的60%调整为基于临界分位数的73%，年节省云开支约15%。

transfer_examples.how: 校招名额分配面临随机到岗率：招多了培训资源浪费(Co)，招少了项目缺人(Cu)。用历史到岗率分布计算最优发offer数量。
transfer_examples.result: 从'多发20%offer'的经验法则转向基于到岗率分位数的精确计算，降低超额招聘成本的同时保证到岗人数达标。

D2.anchor: Little's Law：系统平均在制数量 = 到达率 x 平均停留时间

methods.name: Little's Law (L = λW)
methods.mechanism: 在任何稳态系统中，系统内的平均在制数量(L)等于到达率(λ)乘以平均逗留时间(W)。该关系在极一般的条件下成立——不依赖到达分布、服务分布、排队规则或服务器数量。
methods.input: 三个变量中的任意两个：系统内平均实体数(L)、平均到达率(λ)、平均逗留时间(W)。
methods.process: 1) 确认系统处于稳态（长期统计规律稳定）；2) 测量或获取到达率λ（如每小时订单数）和平均逗留时间W（如从下单到发货的平均时间）；3) 应用 L = λ × W 计算第三个量；4) 反向推导：若已知L和λ，可得W = L/λ，用于估计处理时间而不需直接测量。
methods.output: 系统在制量/到达率/逗留时间三个指标中缺失的那一个，以及稳态约束下的系统容量边界。
methods.key_insight: Little's Law 的威力在于其普遍性——你可以在不了解系统内部任何细节（排队规则、服务时间分布等）的情况下，仅通过外部观测的两个指标推导第三个。
methods.paper: Little, J.D.C. (1961). A Proof for the Queuing Formula: L = λW. Operations Research, 9(3), 383-387.
methods.code: https://simpy.readthedocs.io (SimPy 离散事件仿真框架，可搭建队列模型验证Little's Law)

transfer_examples.how: 将WIP(在制品) = 吞吐率 x 周期时间直接用于看板系统：限制WIP可以降低周期时间（Little's Law 反向推导）。
transfer_examples.result: 团队将WIP限制从12降至6后，平均交付周期从20天缩短至11天，吞吐率基本不变。

transfer_examples.how: 用平均在院患者数(L)和每小时到达患者数(λ)反推平均停留时间W，定位瓶颈环节。
transfer_examples.result: 发现急诊W过高的根因是检验报告等待时间而非医生诊疗时间，将检验流程并行化后W缩短30%。

D2.anchor: PID 的积分项会累积历史误差；anti-windup 在执行器饱和时限制积分继续膨胀

methods.name: PID Integral Anti-Windup
methods.mechanism: PID控制器的积分项(I)持续累积历史误差；当执行器达到物理极限（饱和）后，系统无法进一步响应，但误差仍在累积，导致积分项'卷绕'到极大值。当误差反向时，已累积的巨大积分项造成严重的超调和长时间振荡。Anti-windup在检测到饱和时限制积分继续增长。
methods.input: PID控制器的当前误差e(t)、积分累积值I(t)、执行器饱和上下限(u_min, u_max)、未限幅的控制器输出u_raw。
methods.process: 1) 计算PID未限幅输出 u_raw = Kp*e + Ki*∫e + Kd*(de/dt)；2) 施加执行器限幅：u_out = clamp(u_raw, u_min, u_max)；3) 计算饱和偏差 sat_error = u_raw - u_out；4) Anti-windup核心：若 sat_error ≠ 0，则用 back-calculation 回退积分项或暂停积分累加（conditional integration）；5) 持续运行，确保误差反向时积分项不会因历史卷绕导致大幅超调。
methods.output: 限幅后的控制输出u_out、经anti-windup修正后的积分项I(t)、系统响应曲线（无overshoot或显著降低的overshoot）。
methods.key_insight: 积分项的'好记性'在执行器受限时变成'坏记性'——anti-windup的本质是让控制器感知物理约束并选择性遗忘在饱和期间累积的无效误差。
methods.paper: Åström, K.J. & Hägglund, T. (1995). PID Controllers: Theory, Design, and Tuning. 2nd ed. ISA; See also Åström & Hägglund (2006). Advanced PID Control. ISA.

transfer_examples.how: 将长期压力累积类比为积分卷绕：持续面对不可控压力源（执行器饱和），负面情绪不断累积；'anti-windup'策略是主动识别自己处于'饱和'状态并暂停自我批评。
transfer_examples.result: 在认知行为疗法中引入'saturation awareness'练习，在无法行动时不加重内疚累积，缩短情绪恢复时间。

transfer_examples.how: 团队持续未达OKR但资源已满载（执行器饱和），管理层继续'积分'未完成目标形成惩罚性考核。Anti-windup即在资源饱和时冻结历史未完成项的累积。
transfer_examples.result: 在季度中期发现资源瓶颈后暂停目标累积考核，聚焦优先级调整，避免团队因历史欠账陷入士气螺旋下降。

D2.anchor: 阈值模型里，每个人在看到足够比例他人行动后才加入集体行动

methods.name: Threshold Model of Collective Behavior
methods.mechanism: 群体中每个个体都有一个'参与阈值'——当观察到已参与行动的他人比例达到该阈值时，个体才会加入。相同的阈值分布下，微小的初始条件差异可导致完全不同的集体结果（无人行动 vs. 全员行动）。
methods.input: 群体规模N、每个人的参与阈值分布T(i)、初始已参与人数（种子参与者数量与身份）。
methods.process: 1) 为每个个体分配阈值（可从经验分布采样或实验测量）；2) 设定初始种子参与者（阈值为0的天然行动者）；3) 迭代推进：每轮检查当前未参与者，若已参与人数占比 ≥ 其阈值，则该个体加入行动；4) 记录级联过程：参与者比例随时间的变化曲线、是否达到临界质量引发全面参与；5) 敏感性分析：改变1-2个种子参与者的阈值身份，观察结果是否翻转。
methods.output: 集体行动的级联曲线（参与率 vs 时间）、是否触发全面参与的二元结果、以及对初始条件的敏感度地图。
methods.key_insight: 看似相同的群体可能因一两个'先行者'的阈值差异产生天壤之别——宏观结果对微观阈值分布的尾部极度敏感，这解释了为何预测集体行为如此困难。
methods.paper: Granovetter, M. (1978). Threshold Models of Collective Behavior. American Journal of Sociology, 83(6), 1420-1443.
methods.code: https://github.com/complexity-garage/threshold-model (可自行实现：迭代检查参与率 vs 个体阈值)

transfer_examples.how: 将社交产品的'加入决策'建模为阈值模型：用户看到朋友圈有足够多人使用后才注册。通过降低有效阈值（如提供独立价值，不依赖社交证明）或精准寻找低阈值早期用户启动级联。
transfer_examples.result: 在产品冷启动阶段优先获取阈值为0的种子用户（KOL/早期布道者），而非广撒网式推广。

transfer_examples.how: 将组织变革的采纳建模为阈值扩散：多数员工在看到足够比例同事接受新流程后才愿意切换。变革失败往往因为未达到临界质量。
transfer_examples.result: 识别并培养不同部门的'低阈值'变革拥护者，确保各部门都有可见的先行者，避免某个部门因阈值分布偏高而成为级联断裂点。

D2.anchor: 二价拍卖让最高出价者获胜但只付第二高价，降低虚报价格的动机

methods.name: Vickrey Second-Price Auction (Incentive Compatibility)
methods.mechanism: 在密封投标拍卖中，让出价最高者获胜但只支付第二高的出价。此机制下，诚实报价成为每个竞拍者的占优策略——虚报高价不会多付（支付由他人出价决定），虚报低价只会减少自己的获胜概率而不改变支付金额。
methods.input: 每个竞拍者的私有估值v_i、竞拍者数量N、拍卖规则（密封、最高者赢、付第二高价）。
methods.process: 1) 所有竞拍者密封提交出价b_i；2) 确定最高出价者：winner = argmax(b_i)；3) 确定支付价格：p = 第二高的出价；4) 分析激励相容性：对任意竞拍者i，当b_i = v_i时的期望收益 ≥ 任何b_i ≠ v_i时的期望收益（占优策略）；5) 将结果推广为收入等价定理：在标准条件下，四种标准拍卖形式的期望收入相等。
methods.output: 拍卖赢家、成交价格、各竞拍者的最优策略分析、以及不同拍卖机制间的收入等价性论证。
methods.key_insight: 好的机制设计不是让人'无法欺骗'，而是让人'不想欺骗'——二价拍卖将诚实报价变为自利行为，从而消除了策略性操纵的动机。
methods.paper: Vickrey, W. (1961). Counterspeculation, Auctions, and Competitive Sealed Tenders. The Journal of Finance, 16(1), 8-37.

transfer_examples.how: 将部门间稀缺资源（如云配额、招聘HC）的分配设计为Vickrey式竞价：各部门报出资源对自身的估值，最高者获得资源但按第二高估值付费。诚实报价成为最优策略。
transfer_examples.result: 资源分配从政治博弈转向真实需求驱动，减少过度争抢和战略性的低报/高报。

transfer_examples.how: Google Ads 等搜索引擎广告的核心定价机制即广义二价拍卖(GSP)：广告主按点击出价，实际按第二高出价计费。
transfer_examples.result: 广告主无需精确猜竞争对手出价，只需按自身真实点击价值出价，系统实现高效分配和收入优化。

D2.anchor: 随机对照试验先预定义分组和终点，再用随机化隔离干预效果

methods.name: Randomized Controlled Trial (RCT)
methods.mechanism: 通过随机分配受试者到干预组和对照组，在大样本下使已知和未知混杂因素在组间均匀分布，从而将观察到的因果差异归因于干预本身而非混杂变量。
methods.input: 明确的研究问题、待检验的干预措施、可测量的主要终点指标、受试者纳入/排除标准、预计算的样本量。
methods.process: 1) 预先注册研究方案：定义假设、主要终点、样本量计算、分析计划；2) 受试者筛选和知情同意；3) 随机化分组（简单随机、分层随机或区组随机）；4) 实施干预，对受试者/评估者尽可能设盲（单盲/双盲）；5) 按方案收集终点数据；6) 按预注册分析计划进行统计检验（意向治疗分析ITT为首选）；7) 报告效应量和置信区间，而非仅报告p值。
methods.output: 干预的平均处理效应(ATE)及其置信区间、统计显著性、以及预设亚组分析结果。
methods.key_insight: 随机化的力量不在于'消除'混杂，而在于'平衡'已知和未知混杂——它是唯一能在实验前声称已控制所有混杂因素（包括研究者未想到的）的方法。
methods.paper: Fisher, R.A. (1935). The Design of Experiments. Oliver and Boyd, Edinburgh.

transfer_examples.how: 将RCT方法应用于互联网产品功能测试：随机分流用户到新旧版本，按预定义核心指标评估。关键转移是预注册分析计划以避免'数据窥探'。
transfer_examples.result: 从'发布后看数据'转向'发布前定好统计决策规则'，减少对噪声结果的过拟合决策。

transfer_examples.how: 将RCT应用于学校教育干预（如新教学法）：随机选择学校/班级作为实验组和对照组，标准化测试成绩为主要终点。
transfer_examples.result: 克服了'好学校本来就选了好的教学法'的混杂偏倚，为政策推广提供可信因果证据。

D2.anchor: FMEA 逐项列失效模式、影响、原因和检测方式，再按风险优先级处理

methods.name: Failure Mode and Effects Analysis (FMEA)
methods.mechanism: 系统性地遍历产品/流程的每个组件，识别所有可能的失效模式、分析其对系统的影响和根本原因，评估检测难度，最后按风险优先级数(RPN = 严重度 x 发生度 x 检测度)排序处理。
methods.input: 产品/流程的完整组件清单或流程步骤、每个组件的功能描述、历史失效数据（如有）。
methods.process: 1) 将系统分解为组件/步骤，明确每个的功能和性能要求；2) 对每个组件头脑风暴所有可能的失效模式；3) 分析每个失效模式的局部影响、上一级影响和最终用户影响；4) 识别每个失效模式的潜在根本原因；5) 为每个失效模式评分：严重度S(1-10)、发生度O(1-10)、检测度D(1-10)；6) 计算 RPN = S x O x D；7) 按RPN降序排列，优先处理高RPN项，制定预防/检测措施；8) 实施后重新评估RPN，确认风险降低。
methods.output: 完整的FMEA工作表（失效模式清单含S/O/D评分和RPN排序）、高风险项的纠正措施计划、措施实施后的残余RPN。
methods.key_insight: FMEA的价值不仅在'找到高RPN风险'，更在过程本身——它迫使跨职能团队在设计阶段就直面失败的可能性，将隐性知识转化为显性的风险地图。
methods.paper: MIL-STD-1629A (1980). Procedures for Performing a Failure Mode, Effects, and Criticality Analysis. U.S. DoD; IEC 60812:2018. Failure modes and effects analysis (FMEA and FMECA). International Electrotechnical Commission.
methods.code: https://github.com/search?q=fmea+python (社区FMEA工具); https://github.com/MatthewReid854/reliability (可靠性工程Python库)

transfer_examples.how: 将FMEA应用于新版本发布前：列出每个功能模块、识别可能的失效模式（崩溃、数据丢失、性能退化）、评估严重度/发生概率/可监控性，按RPN排序决定测试重点。
transfer_examples.result: 从'全面测试所有功能'转向'优先深度测试高RPN模块'，在有限测试资源下最大化缺陷拦截率。

transfer_examples.how: 将大型活动（会议、发布会）的各环节按FMEA方法逐一评估：场地设备故障、讲者缺席、网络中断、签到系统崩溃等失效模式，计算RPN并预案。
transfer_examples.result: 提前准备了RPN最高的3个风险的应急方案（备用场地网络、讲者远程接入方案、手动签到流程），活动当天因有预案而从容应对突发状况。

D2.anchor: CRISPR 扰动和空间转录组同时读出，让“做了什么干预”和“组织邻域里发生什么后果”在同一实验里连接

methods.name: Perturb-map: spatially resolved in vivo CRISPR screening
methods.mechanism: CRISPR guide RNAs (sgRNAs) are designed to be detectable via spatial barcoding or in situ hybridization, so each cell's perturbation identity is read out alongside its transcriptome in intact tissue sections. The method pairs pooled CRISPRi/CRISPRa perturbations with spatial transcriptomics (e.g., MERFISH or sequencing-based spatial readout), preserving the neighborhood context that dissociated single-cell screens destroy.
methods.input: Pooled sgRNA library transduced into cells within a tissue (e.g., tumor microenvironment); intact tissue sections for spatial capture.
methods.process: (1) Deliver pooled CRISPR library in vivo so cells carry distinct perturbations. (2) Section tissue and perform spatial transcriptomics that simultaneously captures endogenous gene expression and sgRNA identity per cell. (3) Map each cell to its spatial coordinates and assign its perturbation. (4) Analyze how perturbation of gene X changes the transcriptome and also the neighborhood composition (immune infiltration, cell-type mixing, spatial clustering).
methods.output: Per-cell, per-perturbation spatial gene expression maps; quantification of how each genetic perturbation alters tissue organization, cell-cell communication patterns, and neighborhood-level phenotypes.
methods.key_insight: Classical Perturb-seq reads out perturbation effects after tissue dissociation, losing all spatial context. Perturb-map keeps the tissue intact so that the same CRISPR perturbation can be linked to both cell-intrinsic transcriptomic changes and cell-extrinsic neighborhood remodeling — revealing that perturbation effects are partly determined by where a cell sits.
methods.paper: Feldman D, Singh A, Garrity AJ, et al. 'Spatial CRISPRi screens reveal regulators of the tumor microenvironment.' Science (2022).

transfer_examples.how: Treat policy interventions (e.g., new green space, transit stop) as 'perturbations' and measure both direct effects on targeted blocks and spillover effects on adjacent blocks, analogous to reading out perturbation + neighborhood.
transfer_examples.result: A spatial causal inference framework that distinguishes intervention effects from neighborhood contagion, enabling planners to anticipate second-order spatial impacts before scaling.

transfer_examples.how: When introducing a genetically engineered microbe into a gut or soil microbiome, simultaneously track the engineered organism's gene expression and its spatial neighborhood composition in situ, borrowing Perturb-map's dual-readout design.
transfer_examples.result: Reveals whether an engineered microbe's intended function is altered by its local microbial neighbors, guiding chassis and spatial-delivery design.

D2.anchor: 单细胞和空间组学把细胞身份、状态和空间邻域一起建模，样本不是孤立点而是带邻居的状态点

methods.name: Spatial Transcriptomics（空间转录组学）
methods.mechanism: 不把每个细胞当作孤立的数据点。把细胞的基因表达特征和它在组织中的空间位置、邻居细胞的类型一起建模。同一个细胞在不同邻居环境中可能表现出完全不同的身份和状态。
methods.input: 组织切片的基因表达矩阵 + 空间坐标
methods.process: 每个细胞的特征 = 自身表达 + 邻居表达的加权组合 → 聚类/分类
methods.output: 考虑空间上下文的细胞类型/状态注释
methods.key_insight: 细胞的身份不由自身单独决定，而由自身 + 邻居共同决定
methods.paper: Ståhl et al., Visualization and analysis of gene expression in tissue sections by spatial transcriptomics, Science 2016; Marx, Method of the Year: spatially resolved transcriptomics, Nature Methods 2021
methods.code: squidpy (Python); Scanpy + spatial tools; 10x Genomics Space Ranger

transfer_examples.how: GNN 中每个节点的表征由自身特征 + 邻居节点特征聚合而成
transfer_examples.result: GNN 的核心思想与空间转录组学的邻域建模完全同构

transfer_examples.how: 用户的偏好不只由自身行为决定，还由相似用户的行为决定
transfer_examples.result: ItemCF/UserCF 的核心假设

transfer_examples.how: 一个区域的活力由自身设施 + 周边区域的人流/功能共同决定
transfer_examples.result: 城市活力分析的空间统计方法

D2.anchor: eDNA 从水、土壤、空气等环境痕迹识别物种存在，减少直接捕捉或打扰

methods.name: Environmental DNA (eDNA) metabarcoding for biodiversity monitoring
methods.mechanism: Organisms continuously shed DNA into their environment (mucus, feces, skin cells, decomposing tissue). This environmental DNA can be extracted from water, soil, or air samples, amplified using universal primers targeting conserved marker genes (e.g., mitochondrial 12S, 16S, COI), and sequenced with high-throughput platforms to identify species present without ever capturing or even seeing the organism.
methods.input: Environmental samples: filtered water (often 1-2 L from a river/lake), soil cores, or air filters from a site of interest.
methods.process: (1) Collect environmental sample and filter/concentrate particulate matter. (2) Extract total DNA from the filter or substrate. (3) PCR-amplify with tagged universal primers targeting a taxonomically informative locus (e.g., 12S for fish, COI for invertebrates). (4) High-throughput sequence the amplicon pool. (5) Assign sequences to taxa by matching against reference databases (e.g., BOLD, GenBank). (6) Interpret presence/absence or relative read abundance as a proxy for species occurrence.
methods.output: Species occurrence lists per sampling site; relative read abundance profiles across taxa; temporal trends in community composition.
methods.key_insight: Traditional biodiversity surveys require expert field teams physically capturing or observing organisms — expensive, invasive, and limited in spatial/temporal coverage. eDNA inverts this: organisms leave molecular traces that can be detected hours to weeks after passage, enabling non-invasive, scalable, and repeatable monitoring across entire watersheds or landscapes.
methods.paper: Taberlet P, Coissac E, Hajibabaei M, Rieseberg LH. 'Environmental DNA.' Molecular Ecology 21(8):1789-1793 (2012).

transfer_examples.how: Sample irrigation water or soil around crop fields for eDNA of known pest species. A positive signal triggers targeted intervention before visible crop damage occurs.
transfer_examples.result: Reduces pesticide use by replacing calendar-based spraying with evidence-based, spatially targeted response, lowering cost and ecological harm.

transfer_examples.how: Airborne eDNA sampling inside buildings to detect mold species, pest presence, or microbial community shifts that signal water damage or HVAC failure — without opening walls or setting traps.
transfer_examples.result: Continuous, non-invasive building diagnostics that catch biohazard conditions early and guide maintenance prioritization.

D2.anchor: VGI/V2G 把电动车同时当作交通工具、可调度负载和移动储能，调度时要兼顾用户出行与电网需求

methods.name: Vehicle-to-Grid (V2G) bidirectional power and energy services
methods.mechanism: An electric vehicle's battery pack is connected to the grid via a bidirectional charger. When parked and plugged in, the vehicle can discharge stored energy back to the grid during peak demand (or absorb excess renewable generation during surplus). The EV simultaneously serves as transportation for its owner and as a distributed grid asset, creating a dual-use scheduling problem: the dispatch algorithm must guarantee the owner's mobility needs while extracting maximum value from grid services (frequency regulation, peak shaving, spinning reserves).
methods.input: Fleet of EVs with bidirectional chargers, grid operator demand/supply signals (real-time pricing, frequency deviation data), vehicle owner departure-time and minimum charge-level preferences.
methods.process: (1) Each vehicle communicates its state-of-charge, plugged-in status, and next-departure constraints to an aggregator. (2) The aggregator solves a constrained optimization that maximizes revenue from grid services (frequency regulation bids, energy arbitrage) subject to each vehicle's minimum charge requirement at departure. (3) Power electronics control charge/discharge rate within battery degradation limits. (4) Revenue is shared between vehicle owner and aggregator via a pricing contract.
methods.output: Aggregated dispatch schedule for the EV fleet; per-vehicle state-of-charge trajectory that meets mobility constraints; grid service capacity (kW) and energy delivered (kWh) per settlement period; revenue allocation to vehicle owners.
methods.key_insight: Kempton and Tomic's core insight was that cars are parked ~95% of the time and their batteries are vastly over-sized relative to daily driving needs. This idle capacity — if aggregated across millions of vehicles — constitutes a grid-scale storage resource comparable to pumped hydro, without building any new infrastructure. The key design challenge is aligning two incentive structures: the grid needs reliability, and the driver needs a charged car.
methods.paper: Kempton W, Tomić J. 'Vehicle-to-grid power fundamentals: Calculating capacity and net revenue.' Journal of Power Sources 144(1):268-279 (2005); companion paper: 'Vehicle-to-grid power implementation: From stabilizing the grid to supporting large-scale renewable energy.' Journal of Power Sources 144(1):280-294 (2005).

transfer_examples.how: Idle devices (smartphones, laptops, IoT gateways) are enrolled as a compute fleet. Each device owner sets availability windows and minimum battery reserve. A scheduler allocates batch compute jobs (e.g., ML inference, data preprocessing) to devices when they are idle and plugged in, mirroring V2G's dual-use scheduling.
transfer_examples.result: Leverages billions of idle device-hours for edge computing without requiring dedicated server infrastructure, while guaranteeing device owners their devices are ready when needed.

transfer_examples.target: Water infrastructure: distributed storage as grid asset
transfer_examples.how: Smart water heaters and residential cisterns are aggregated as a thermal/hydraulic storage fleet, absorbing excess renewable electricity (by heating water or pumping cisterns) and reducing load during peaks, using the same aggregator-optimizer pattern as V2G.
transfer_examples.result: Grid flexibility at near-zero marginal infrastructure cost, with each household's comfort constraints preserved.

D2.anchor: 建筑结构由打印路径、材料配方和层间连接共同生成，不再只由统一模具决定

methods.name: 3D concrete printing (additive manufacturing of cementitious structures)
methods.mechanism: A computer-controlled robotic arm or gantry extrudes a cement-based material through a nozzle, depositing it layer by layer along a toolpath generated from a digital model. Unlike conventional construction where a uniform mold defines the geometry, in 3DCP the structure is co-determined by three coupled variables: (a) the print path (which defines geometry, including curved and non-orthogonal forms), (b) the material mix design (which must be fluid enough to extrude yet stiff enough to hold shape under the weight of subsequent layers), and (c) the interlayer bond quality (each layer must chemically and mechanically bond to the previous one before it sets).
methods.input: 3D CAD/BIM model of the structure; cementitious mix design (cite: cement, sand, fiber, admixtures, water-to-binder ratio); printer kinematics (nozzle diameter, print speed, layer height).
methods.process: (1) Slice the 3D model into layers and generate toolpaths with consideration for structural loading, overhang limits, and material flow. (2) Prepare the printable mix: tune rheology so it is pumpable and extrudable (shear-thinning) but thixotropic enough to support itself after deposition. (3) Print layer by layer; each layer must be deposited within a critical open time window so the bond with the previous layer is chemical (hydrate bridging) rather than just frictional. (4) Optionally insert reinforcement (fiber in mix, or manual rebar/FRP between layers). (5) Post-process: curing, surface finishing, and quality inspection (dimensional accuracy, interlayer bond testing).
methods.output: Completed structural element (wall, column, facade panel) with geometry that can include freeform curves, internal cavities for insulation or services, and variable cross-sections — features difficult or expensive to achieve with conventional formwork.
methods.key_insight: Buswell et al. framed 3DCP not as a replacement for all construction but as a method that unlocks geometries and material efficiencies impossible with formwork-based casting. The critical research frontier is not the printer hardware but the coupling between material science (printable mix rheology), structural engineering (interlayer bond strength, anisotropy), and digital workflow (toolpath optimization for load paths). These three domains must co-evolve; optimizing any one in isolation fails.
methods.paper: Buswell RA, Leal de Silva WR, Jones SZ, Dirrenberger J. '3D printing using concrete extrusion: A roadmap for research.' Cement and Concrete Research 112:37-49 (2018).

transfer_examples.how: 3DCP's ability to print complex, non-repeating geometries with internal porosity is applied to fabricate reef substrate units that mimic natural coral morphology, using calcium-aluminate or geopolymer cements tuned for marine pH.
transfer_examples.result: Artificial reef modules with species-appropriate surface roughness and cavity sizes, produced without molds, enabling site-specific restoration at scale.

transfer_examples.how: A mobile 3DCP unit deploys to a disaster zone and prints shelters, water-channel linings, or bridge abutments using locally sourced aggregate mixed on-site, bypassing formwork logistics that are impractical in damaged infrastructure contexts.
transfer_examples.result: Structures erected in hours instead of weeks, with geometry optimized on-site for local load conditions, reducing dependency on supply chains for standardized building components.

D2.anchor: 循环建材把一处系统的废料重新变成另一处系统的输入，生命周期从“使用后丢弃”改为“再进入系统”

methods.name: Circular building design: design for disassembly and material passporting
methods.mechanism: Buildings are designed from the outset so that every component can be identified, detached, and reused at end of the building's current service life. This requires three interlocking practices: (a) material passports — digital records attached to each component specifying its composition, origin, structural rating, and toxicity profile; (b) reversible connections — bolts, clips, and dry joints replace welds and chemical adhesives so components can be separated without damage; (c) modular hierarchy — the building is organized into layers (structure, skin, services, space plan) with different lifespans, so short-lived layers can be swapped without disturbing long-lived ones.
methods.input: Building design intent; material inventory with environmental and structural properties; disassembly sequence plan; market demand data for reclaimed materials.
methods.process: (1) Design phase: select materials and connection types to enable future disassembly; register every component in a material passport (BIM-linked database). (2) Construction phase: assemble using reversible joints; record as-built passport data. (3) Operation phase: maintain passports as components are repaired or replaced. (4) End-of-service: execute planned disassembly sequence; sort components by passport data for reuse, remanufacture, or high-grade recycling. (5) Reuse: components re-enter the supply chain as inputs for new construction or renovation projects.
methods.output: A building that, at end of service, yields a catalogued stock of reusable components rather than a pile of mixed demolition waste. Material value is preserved across multiple use cycles.
methods.key_insight: The Ellen MacArthur Foundation's circular economy framework, applied to buildings, highlights that the built environment is the largest material stock in any economy — yet >90% of construction materials are downcycled or landfilled after a single use. The shift from 'use-then-dispose' to 'use-then-disassemble-then-reuse' requires the building itself to be designed as a temporary configuration of durable components, not as a monolithic artifact. The material passport is the enabling information layer: without knowing what a component is made of and how it was connected, reuse is impossible.
methods.paper: Ellen MacArthur Foundation, McKinsey Center for Business and Environment. 'Growth Within: Redefining Value in the Circular Economy' (2015); see also: Ellen MacArthur Foundation. 'Towards the Circular Economy' vol. 1 (2013).

transfer_examples.how: Apply the building-layer hierarchy to consumer electronics: the structural frame, battery, screen, and camera module each have a different optimal replacement cycle. Design reversible connectors (e.g., Fairphone-style screw-mount modules) and attach a 'component passport' (material composition, remaining cycle count) to each module.
transfer_examples.result: Users upgrade individual modules rather than replacing the whole device, reducing e-waste and extending the useful life of the most resource-intensive components (frame, PCB).

transfer_examples.target: Fashion industry: garment disassembly and fiber recovery
transfer_examples.how: Design garments with reversible seams (ultrasonic welds that peel, snap-fit tracers) and embed NFC-based material passports in labels. At end-of-use, automated disassembly recovers intact fabric panels for re-cutting or chemical fiber recycling with known composition.
transfer_examples.result: Fiber-to-fiber recycling becomes economically viable because the input stream is pure and documented, eliminating the sorting bottleneck that currently makes textile recycling uneconomic.

D2.anchor: 研究设计过的 AI tutor 在 RCT 中用即时反馈和个性化脚手架提升学习效果，而不是只提供内容库

methods.name: Generative AI tutor with Socratic scaffolding in RCT
methods.mechanism: A large language model (GPT-4) is given a carefully engineered system prompt that enforces Socratic teaching behavior: when a student asks a question or presents a problem, the AI does not give the answer directly. Instead, it asks guiding questions, provides analogies, breaks the problem into sub-steps, and gives targeted hints — mimicking expert human tutoring pedagogy. The RCT design compares this AI-tutored group against both passive-lecture and active-learning (peer instruction) groups, measuring normalized learning gain.
methods.input: Student question or problem statement in an introductory physics context; course content and solution key for calibration; pedagogical prompt template (Socratic rules, scaffolding ladder).
methods.process: (1) Engineer the system prompt to enforce Socratic behavior: no direct answers, use questions to probe student understanding, scaffold from concrete to abstract. (2) Student interacts with the AI tutor in real time during a study session. (3) AI responds with guided questions and hints tailored to the student's stated reasoning. (4) In the RCT, randomly assign students to: (a) AI-tutored passive-lecture supplement, (b) traditional active learning (peer instruction with clickers), (c) passive lecture only. (5) Administer pre/post normalized learning gain assessments.
methods.output: Normalized learning gains per condition; the AI-tutored group showed significantly higher gains than passive lecture alone, though active learning (peer instruction) still outperformed AI-supplemented passive lecture. The key result: AI tutoring closed a substantial portion of the gap between passive and active learning.
methods.key_insight: The critical design choice was not the model itself but the pedagogical prompt — forcing the LLM into a Socratic role rather than an answer-delivery role. This transforms a knowledge-retrieval system into a reasoning-scaffolding system. The RCT evidence shows that well-designed AI tutoring can approach (but not fully replace) the efficacy of human-facilitated active learning, and it dramatically outperforms passive instruction.
methods.paper: Kestin G, Miller K, McCarty LS. 'Generative AI Can Supplement, but Not Replace, Active Learning in Physics Education.' Physical Review Physics Education Research 20(2):020116 (2024).

transfer_examples.target: Medical residency training: diagnostic reasoning practice
transfer_examples.how: Deploy a Socratic AI tutor for residents presenting clinical vignettes. The AI does not diagnose; instead it asks 'What are the key findings? What is your differential? What test would distinguish X from Y?' — scaffolding clinical reasoning through guided questioning.
transfer_examples.result: Residents practice diagnostic reasoning with infinite patience and availability, complementing but not replacing supervised clinical rotations, which remain essential for procedural and interpersonal skill.

transfer_examples.target: Trade skills training: on-the-job troubleshooting coaching
transfer_examples.how: An apprentice electrician or plumber encountering a novel fault describes the symptoms to a Socratic AI coach that asks leading questions about diagnostic steps rather than giving the fix directly.
transfer_examples.result: Accelerates development of troubleshooting intuition (pattern recognition + causal reasoning) while preserving the master-apprentice relationship for hands-on skills and safety-critical judgment.

D2.anchor: Tutor CoPilot 不替代人类导师，而是在实时辅导中给导师专家策略提示

methods.name: Tutor CoPilot: real-time expert strategy prompting for human tutors
methods.mechanism: Rather than replacing the human tutor with an AI, the system observes the live tutoring conversation and generates real-time suggestions to the tutor — expert-level teaching strategies, rephrasings, or scaffolding moves the tutor might not have thought of. The AI acts as a coach-the-coach layer: it draws on a curated library of expert tutoring behaviors (e.g., 'redirect with a question instead of correcting directly', 'use a concrete analogy for this abstract concept') and surfaces the most contextually appropriate suggestion at the moment the tutor is about to respond.
methods.input: Real-time transcript of the live tutoring session (tutor-student dialogue); curated expert strategy library; tutor profile (experience level, subject domain).
methods.process: (1) As the tutor-student conversation streams in, the system parses the dialogue context (student's misconception, tutor's intended response). (2) A retrieval-augmented generation pipeline selects relevant expert strategies from the curated library. (3) The system generates 1-3 suggested response strategies and displays them to the tutor in real time (e.g., in a sidebar). (4) The tutor selects, adapts, or ignores the suggestions and responds to the student. (5) Post-session analytics track which suggestions were adopted and correlate with student outcomes.
methods.output: Tutor responses that more frequently employ expert pedagogical strategies; measured improvement in tutoring quality, especially for less experienced tutors; student learning outcome improvements in the treatment group.
methods.key_insight: The key design insight is that AI assistance for professionals works best as augmentation, not automation. Lower-performing tutors benefit disproportionately from CoPilot suggestions because the system effectively gives them access to expert intuitions they have not yet developed. This suggests a scalable path to educational equity: rather than trying to replace teachers with AI, give every teacher access to expert-level coaching in the moment they need it.
methods.paper: Wang RE, Ribeiro DT, Robinson CD, et al. 'Tutor CoPilot: A Human-AI Approach for Scaling Real-Time Expertise.' NeurIPS 2024.

transfer_examples.target: Emergency dispatch: real-time coaching for 911 operators
transfer_examples.how: As a 911 call progresses, an AI assistant listens to the conversation and surfaces expert protocols and de-escalation strategies to the dispatcher in real time — e.g., 'caller may be in X situation, consider asking about Y' — without taking over the call.
transfer_examples.result: Less experienced dispatchers perform closer to expert-level protocol adherence, reducing critical errors in high-stress, time-sensitive situations.

transfer_examples.target: Clinical nursing: real-time decision support at bedside
transfer_examples.how: A nurse managing a complex patient case receives contextual prompts from an AI that has been trained on expert nursing decision patterns — not replacing clinical judgment, but surfacing considerations (drug interactions, escalation criteria) the nurse might be too overloaded to recall in the moment.
transfer_examples.result: Novel nurses make fewer process errors during high-acuity shifts, narrowing the experience-dependent performance gap without removing the nurse from the decision loop.

D2.anchor: 高风险法律 AI 需要引用验证、责任链和人工复核嵌入工作流，不能只给生成文本

methods.name: Verified legal AI: citation checking, responsibility chains, and human-in-the-loop workflows
methods.mechanism: Legal AI systems operating in high-stakes contexts (litigation, regulatory compliance, judicial decision support) cannot rely on generative text output alone. The method embeds three safeguards: (a) citation verification — every legal claim the AI outputs is automatically cross-checked against a verified legal database (case law, statute) before presentation; (b) responsibility chain — each AI-generated output carries provenance metadata (source document, retrieval score, human reviewer) so accountability is traceable; (c) human-in-the-loop workflow — the AI produces a draft or recommendation, but a qualified legal professional must review, approve, and take responsibility before the output is used in any consequential action.
methods.input: Legal query or task (e.g., draft a motion, research precedent for a claim); verified legal corpus (e.g., Westlaw, LexisNexis, court records); organizational compliance rules defining which tasks require human sign-off.
methods.process: (1) User poses a legal question or task to the system. (2) The AI retrieves relevant sources from a verified legal database using RAG (retrieval-augmented generation), not free generation from parametric memory. (3) The AI drafts a response with inline citations linked to specific source passages. (4) An automated citation verifier checks that every cited case exists, the quoted passage is accurate, and the legal holding is correctly characterized. (5) The draft, with verification status flagged, is presented to a human attorney for review, editing, and approval. (6) The approved output, along with its full provenance chain, is logged for audit.
methods.output: A legal document or research memo where every citation is verified as real and accurate; full provenance trail showing which sources were used, what the AI suggested, and what the human approved; compliance audit log.
methods.key_insight: The Mata v. Avianca incident (2023) — where a lawyer submitted ChatGPT-fabricated case citations to a federal court — crystallized the risk: generative models can produce authoritative-sounding but entirely fictitious legal references. The response is not to ban AI from legal practice, but to architect systems where the AI is never the final authority: it drafts, it cites, but every citation is machine-verified and every output passes through a human who bears professional responsibility. The EU AI Act (2024) formalizes this by classifying judicial/legal AI as high-risk, requiring transparency, logging, and human oversight.
methods.paper: Magesh V, Surani F, Dahl M, Márquez C, Ho DE. 'Hallucination-Free? Assessing the Reliability of Leading AI Legal Research Tools.' Stanford HAI / RegLab (2024). EU AI Act, Regulation (EU) 2024/1689.

transfer_examples.target: Medical AI: drug interaction checking with provenance
transfer_examples.how: A clinical decision support AI suggests a medication regimen; every stated interaction is automatically verified against a pharmacological database (e.g., DrugBank) with source passage highlighted, and the output requires physician sign-off before entering the order.
transfer_examples.result: Prevents AI hallucinated drug interactions from reaching the patient while still accelerating the physician's information synthesis, especially for polypharmacy cases.

transfer_examples.target: Scientific peer review: automated claim verification
transfer_examples.how: An AI reviewer drafts comments on a submitted paper; each factual claim it makes (e.g., 'this method was first proposed by X') is cross-verified against the citation database. The human editor reviews the verified draft before sending it to authors.
transfer_examples.result: Reduces reviewer workload for factual-checking while maintaining the scientific community's standard that human experts bear responsibility for review judgments.

D2.anchor: 多智能体市场要设计资源分配、偏好协调和系统性风险规则，而不是只优化单个 agent

methods.name: Multi-agent market mechanism design: resource allocation, preference coordination, and systemic risk governance
methods.mechanism: In multi-agent systems where autonomous agents (AI or human) compete for shared resources, the market mechanism itself must be explicitly designed to achieve three goals simultaneously: (a) efficient resource allocation — items or capacities go to agents who value them most; (b) preference coordination — agents with conflicting or complementary preferences reach mutually acceptable outcomes through bidding, negotiation, or matching protocols; (c) systemic risk mitigation — the mechanism prevents cascading failures where one agent's bankruptcy or misallocation propagates through the network (analogous to financial contagion). This differs from single-agent optimization, where the designer's task is to optimize one objective; here, the designer optimizes the rules of the game, not any individual player's outcome.
methods.input: Agent population with heterogeneous preferences, endowments, and constraints; resource supply profiles; network topology of inter-agent dependencies; risk tolerance thresholds for the system designer.
methods.process: (1) Define the mechanism: auction format (sealed-bid, ascending, Vickrey), matching protocol, or negotiation rules. (2) Model agents as strategic entities (possibly LLM-based or MARL-based) that optimize their own utility within the mechanism's rules. (3) Simulate agent interactions at scale, observing emergent allocation outcomes, price dynamics, and failure cascades. (4) Evaluate the mechanism on three axes: allocative efficiency (total surplus), fairness (distributional equity across agents), and systemic resilience (does the system survive the failure of N agents?). (5) Iteratively redesign the mechanism (adjust reserve prices, add circuit breakers, change information disclosure rules) until all three objectives are satisficed.
methods.output: A market mechanism (set of rules, protocols, and parameters) that achieves near-efficient allocation while bounding systemic risk; simulation evidence of mechanism performance under stress scenarios; formal guarantees on properties like strategy-proofness or budget balance where achievable.
methods.key_insight: Traditional mechanism design (Myerson, Vickrey) focuses on single-item or single-round optimality. Multi-agent markets with AI participants introduce two new challenges: (1) agents can be arbitrarily sophisticated strategists (LLM agents can reason about the mechanism itself), and (2) networked interdependencies mean that local efficiency can produce global fragility. The designer must therefore treat the market as a complex adaptive system: optimizing for equilibrium properties (efficiency, incentive compatibility) is necessary but not sufficient — the mechanism must also be robust to off-equilibrium dynamics and cascading failures.
methods.paper: Multiple 2024-2025 papers on LLM-agent auctions (e.g., 'Can Large Language Models Design Auctions?', 'EconAgent' / 'LLM-Econ' frameworks); foundational theory: Myerson RB. 'Optimal Auction Design.' Mathematics of Operations Research 6(1):58-73 (1981); network systemic risk: Eisenberg L, Noe T. 'Systemic Risk in Financial Systems.' Management Science 47(2):236-249 (2001).

transfer_examples.how: Design a spot-instance auction mechanism for cloud compute that allocates GPU/CPU capacity to competing AI training jobs while incorporating circuit breakers that prevent a single dominant buyer from cornering the market (systemic concentration risk) and reserve capacity guarantees for smaller tenants.
transfer_examples.result: Higher utilization of cloud infrastructure with bounded market concentration, preventing the 'winner-take-all' dynamics that currently make AI compute access inequitable.

transfer_examples.target: Urban water allocation under climate stress
transfer_examples.how: Apply multi-agent market design to allocate scarce water rights among agricultural, industrial, and residential users. The mechanism includes preference elicitation (users reveal valuations via iterative bidding), allocation rules that guarantee minimum residential supply (fairness constraint), and drought-triggered rationing protocols (systemic risk mitigation).
transfer_examples.result: Water is allocated to highest-value uses during normal conditions while residential supply is protected during droughts, replacing politically contentious annual allocations with an adaptive, transparent market protocol.

D2.source: [Adapting a Physical Earthquake-Aftershock Model to Simulate the Spread of COVID-19](https://www.semanticscholar.org/paper/db72bf2280b2ae80f2b3c024571896ad23547b78)

This model is grafted onto COVID-19 by treating infection pressure propagation analogously to stress diffusion, where the pandemic spreads through
methods.output: 目标域: epidemiology 的解决方案

D2.anchor: Hydraulic-analogy bioenergetic models：Applies hydraulic tank models from hydrology, where energy stores are fluid volumes in tanks with flow governed by level differences and pipe capacities, to model
D2.source: [Validity and Reliability of Hydraulic-Analogy Bioenergetic Models in Sprint Roller Skiing](https://www.semanticscholar.org/paper/5d0422e9c07b52b66f28a5a0b20859feebca9a1e)

methods.mechanism: Applies hydraulic tank models from hydrology, where energy stores are fluid volumes in tanks with flow governed by level differences and pipe capacities, to model aerobic, glycolytic, and phosphagen contributions in biomechanics of human exercise performance.

methods.paper: Validity and Reliability of Hydraulic-Analogy Bioenergetic Models in Sprint Roller Skiing

D2.anchor: Geophysics-inspired nonlinear stress-strain law for tissues：Applies a nonlinear stress-strain law derived from seismological principles of wave propagation in heterogeneous media to model the nonlinea
D2.source: [Geophysics-Inspired Nonlinear Stress–Strain Law for Biological Tissues and Its Applications in Compression Optical Coherence Elastography](https://www.semanticscholar.org/paper/54eeb310ecdb8317f65d1ed06b3cec31e469e2f6)

methods.mechanism: Applies a nonlinear stress-strain law derived from seismological principles of wave propagation in heterogeneous media to model the nonlinear elastic properties of biological tissues in biomechanics.
methods.input: 源域: Geophysics
methods.process: The paper models interstitial gaps and pores in biological tissues as analogous to cracks in rocks. Just as narrow cracks in rocks are highly compliant and close progressively under compressive stress (with smaller aspect ratio cracks closing at lower pressures), the interstitial gaps in tissues clo
methods.output: 目标域: biological tissue mechanics 的解决方案

methods.paper: Geophysics-Inspired Nonlinear Stress–Strain Law for Biological Tissues and Its Applications in Compression Optical Coherence Elastography

D2.anchor: Horton-Strahler laws for blood vessels：Applies fractal geometry and fractal dimension concepts from river networks and urban hierarchies (network theory analogs) to analyze and quantify the self-simil
D2.source: [Fractals and fractal dimension of systems of blood vessels: An analogy between artery trees, river networks, and urban hierarchies](https://arxiv.org/abs/1511.02276)

methods.mechanism: Applies fractal geometry and fractal dimension concepts from river networks and urban hierarchies (network theory analogs) to analyze and quantify the self-similar branching structure of arterial trees in vascular biology.

methods.process: The paper applies Horton-Strahler's stream ordering system from river networks to blood vessels, creating three exponential composition laws: the bifurcation ratio (ratio of vessels between consecutive orders), the length ratio (ratio of average vessel lengths), and the diameter ratio (ratio of aver
methods.output: 目标域: blood vessels 的解决方案

In the SN1-like sequential mechanism, genes first silence to destabilize the initial phenotype (like breaking a bond first), then activate to form the new phenotype (like forming a

The 'birth' events correspond to excitatory inputs that increase the membrane potential, while 'death' e
methods.output: 目标域: computational neuroscience 的解决方案

In this application, cells are represented as extended domains on a lattice (analogous to grains in poly
